title: APSDMA warns of thunderstorms, severe heatwave across Andhra - The New Indian Express
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andhra Pradesh
full_text:
VIJAYAWADA: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) has warned of varied weather conditions across the state, with thunderstorms, lightning in some districts and extreme heat in others.
MD Prakhar Jain said a trough extending from East Vidarbha to South Kerala, passing through Telangana, Rayalaseema, South Interior Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry, is influencing the weather. Moderate to heavy rains with lightning and gusty winds of 40–50 kmph are likely in parts of the state on Wednesday.
For Tuesday, June 2, SDMA forecast moderate to heavy rains with lightning in Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla and Palnadu districts. Light to moderate rains are expected in Sathya Sai, Annamayya and Chittoor, while light rains with lightning may occur in Manyam, Alluri, Visakhapatnam, Anakapalli, West Godavari, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore, Anantapur, Kadapa, Tirupati.
The authority urged farmers, cattle herders and outdoor workers to remain vigilant. People are advised not to take shelter under trees, electric poles or hoardings during thunderstorms, and to move to safe buildings if skies turn overcast. Citizens should also avoid snapped power lines that may fall due to strong winds.
In Polavaram, Eluru, NTR, Guntur and Palnadu districts, maximum temperatures are expected to reach 42-44°C. In Manyam, Kakinada, Konaseema, East Godavari, Krishna, Bapatla, Markapuram, Prakasam and Nellore, temperatures may rise to 40-42°C.
On Monday, June 1, extreme temperatures were recorded: 43.6°C in Gudur (Nellore), 43.3°C in Nandanamarellu (Markapuram), 43.1°C in Venkatagiri (Tirupati), 42.2°C in Sindhurajapuram (Chittoor), 41.5°C in Macherla (Palnadu), and 41°C in Sanjamala (Nandyal) and Kanaganapalli (Sri Sathya Sai).

title: Telangana and Andhra Pradesh Reel Under Heatwave as Delayed Monsoon Deepens Distress - The Morning Voice
full_text:
Telangana and Andhra Pradesh Reel Under Heatwave as Delayed Monsoon Deepens Distress
Both Telangana and Andhra Pradesh are enduring a fierce combination of extreme heat and erratic pre-monsoon storms on June 2, as the southwest monsoon continues to remain absent from the Telugu states. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a formal weather advisory covering virtually all districts in Telangana and several coastal and inland zones of Andhra Pradesh.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Centre has placed the entirety of Telangana under a Yellow Alert for thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds of 40–50 kmph, covering all major districts including Hyderabad, Rangareddy, Warangal, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Nalgonda, and Adilabad. Daytime temperatures across the state are expected to hover between 41°C and 45°C , with heat stress remaining critically high through afternoon hours. Nirmal recorded 46.5°C, Hanamkonda 46.4°C, Jagityal 46.3°C, and Adilabad 46.2°C in recent readings. These severe conditions are forecast to persist at least until June 3.
In Andhra Pradesh, the State Disaster Management Authority has escalated alerts in select districts. Orange alerts have been declared for NTR, Prakasam, and Nellore districts, while Krishna, Guntur, and Bapatla have been placed under lightning warnings. Districts such as Parvathipuram Manyam, East Godavari, Kadapa, and Tirupati are likely to record temperatures between 40°C and 42°C, with Achampet in Palnadu district having recently touched 44.3°C. Sporadic thunderstorms with gusty winds are expected across Anantapur, Chittoor, and Markapuram.
Several active weather systems are influencing the region, a trough extending from central Pakistan to north coastal Andhra Pradesh, a cyclonic circulation over the Bay of Bengal , and another trough from the Lakshadweep area, together producing dense cloud cover across multiple atmospheric levels.
The delayed arrival of the monsoon remains the central concern. The IMD has revised its forecast, now expecting the monsoon to reach Kerala between June 4 and June 5 , nearly four to five days behind the usual June 1 onset. Weather experts caution that its advance into Telangana and Andhra Pradesh is unlikely before June 10, and there are indications it may arrive weakened, reducing initial rainfall intensity across the region.
The broader seasonal outlook offers little comfort. Below-normal rainfall is projected for the June-September monsoon season, with both states at elevated risk for above-normal heatwave days. Authorities have urged residents to avoid outdoor exposure during peak afternoon hours (12 PM-4 PM), stay adequately hydrated, and remain vigilant against lightning during sudden evening storms. Farmers have been advised to monitor IMD advisories closely given the risk of erratic gusty winds damaging standing crops.

title: APSDMA flags rain, thunderstorms amid heatwave - The Hans India
full_text:
APSDMA flags rain, thunderstorms amid heatwave
Vijayawada: Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) Managing Director Prakhar Jain has said that the state is likely to experience varied weather conditions, including thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and rainfall in several areas, while heat conditions may continue in some districts.
According to the APSDMA, a trough extending from East Vidarbha to South Kerala across Telangana, Rayalaseema, South Interior Karnataka, Tamil Nadu and Puducherry is influencing the current weather pattern. As a result, moderate to heavy rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning is expected in several parts of the state over the next few days.
Jain stated that gusty winds with speeds of 40-50 kmph are also likely to occur, and people have been advised to remain vigilant.
Moderate to heavy rain accompanied by thunderstorms is likely at isolated places in Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla and Palnadu districts.
Light to moderate rain with thunderstorms is expected in Sri Sathya Sai, Annamayya and Chittoor districts.
Meanwhile, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitarama Raju, Visakhapatnam, Anakapalli, West Godavari, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore, Anantapur, YSR Kadapa and Tirupati districts may receive light rain accompanied by lightning at isolated places.
Farmers and cattle rearers have been advised to exercise extra caution and move to safe locations immediately when the sky turns cloudy and thunder is heard. People should also stay away from fallen or damaged electrical wires that may have snapped due to strong winds and rain.
Despite the rainfall forecast, high temperatures are expected in several districts on Tuesday, June 2.
Temperatures between 42°C and 44°C are likely in parts of Polavaram, Eluru, NTR, Guntur and Palnadu districts.
Temperatures ranging from 40°C to 42°C may be recorded in parts of Parvathipuram Manyam, Kakinada, Konaseema, East Godavari, Krishna, Bapatla, Markapuram, Prakasam and Nellore districts.
On Monday, the highest temperatures recorded in the state were 43.6°C at Gudur in Nellore district, 43.3°C at Nandanamarella in Markapuram district, 43.1°C at Venkatagiri in Tirupati district, 42.2°C at Sindhurajapuram in Chittoor district, 41.5°C at Macherla in Palnadu district, and 41°C each at Sanjamala in Nandyal district and Kanaganapalli in Sri Sathya Sai district.
